summary: Iteration 91: Trust Badge marketing landing page (/trust-badge) and Stripe checkout integration. 10/10 backend pytest passed (subscription checkout for pro/enterprise/free/invalid + auth + verify regression). Frontend public load, unauth redirect, all customizer interactions (style, business name, verified toggle, snippet), SVG live preview, and /verify cross-link verified end-to-end. 0 console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: TrustBadgeLanding.jsx is well structured (~340 lines) and split BadgePreview as inline component — acceptable size.
checkout() uses localStorage.getItem('token'); consistent with the rest of the app. Auth guard correctly redirects to /login?next=/trust-badge when no token.
STYLE_PALETTES is defined as a module constant (not re-built on render) — good perf.
BadgePreview SVG renders fully client-side, no API call — confirmed by network log.
Snippet block correctly omits data-style param when style==='default' — verified.

Hero copy says '$29/mo' but Pro price card and backend plan price both show $49/mo. The headline copy ($29) is INCONSISTENT with actual Stripe checkout amount ($49) — this is a marketing copy bug, not a checkout bug. Recommend fixing the hero/CTA copy to '$49/mo' or aligning the plan price to $29 for the Trust-Badge-only tier.

subscription_routes.py:342 — 'You are already on this plan' check uses exact plan match. If user is enterprise and tries to checkout pro (downgrade), the check passes. Confirmed admin (enterprise) can create a pro checkout session — semantically odd but matches existing behavior.
MongoDB _id correctly excluded throughout subscription_routes.py via projection or by not returning the doc.
test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! (enterprise) and demo@test.com / Demo123! (free) — both produced valid Stripe checkout sessions for plan_id=pro.
seed_data_creation: Pending Stripe checkout sessions created (no payment completed) — recorded in db.subscription_payments with status='pending'. No cleanup needed; abandoned sessions are normal Stripe behavior.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test at /app/backend/tests/test_trust_badge_landing.py covers full /api/subscriptions/checkout matrix + /verify regression. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_trust_badge_landing.py -v. /trust-badge route is public (lazy-loaded). All key data-testids present: trust-badge-landing-page, hero-pro-checkout-btn, pricing-pro-btn, pricing-enterprise-btn, final-cta-btn, preview-business-input, preview-style-{default|dark|light|minimal}, preview-toggle-verified, badge-svg-preview, preview-snippet, trust-badge-cta (on /verify). Hero copy still says $29/mo while actual Stripe charge is $49/mo (Pro plan price) — flag as inconsistency.
verified_flows: GET /trust-badge (public, no auth) — 200, trust-badge-landing-page testid visible, full layout renders (hero, proof, customizer, how-it-works, use cases, pricing, FAQ, final CTA)
Click hero-pro-checkout-btn WITHOUT auth → window.location → /login?next=/trust-badge
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout {plan_id: 'pro', origin_url} as demo user (free tier) → 200, checkout_url starts with https://checkout.stripe.com, valid session_id, plan.id='pro', plan.price=49.0
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout {plan_id: 'enterprise'} as admin (already enterprise) → 400 'You are already on this plan'
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout {plan_id: 'pro'} as admin → 200 with stripe URL (admin can downgrade-checkout)
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout {plan_id: 'free'} → 400 'Free plan does not require payment'
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout {plan_id: 'bogus'} → 400 'Invalid plan selected'
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout without auth → 401/403
preview-business-input typing → live SVG text node updates with new business name
preview-style-{default|dark|light|minimal} buttons all click-able and switch palette colors
preview-snippet contains data-style="{style}" param when non-default style; omits param for default style
preview-toggle-verified flips between '✓ Verified state' and '⚠ Pending state'
badge-svg-preview <svg> renders without backend call (pure client-side, no network requests)
Final CTA, pricing-pro-btn, pricing-enterprise-btn data-testids all present and rendered
/verify footer cross-link 'Get your badge →' has data-testid='trust-badge-cta' and href='/trust-badge'
Console errors during /trust-badge full interaction: 0
Console errors on /verify: 0
Regression: GET /api/verify/document/{invalid_hash} → 400; valid 64-char unknown hash → 200 verified=false
Regression: GET /api/verify/widget.js → 200, application/javascript
Regression: GET /api/subscriptions/plans → 200 with all 3 plans (free/pro/enterprise)
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Only soft observation: the hero CTA says '$29/mo' while the actual Pro plan price (and the Stripe checkout amount and the pricing card) is $49/mo. This is a copywriting inconsistency the marketer/product owner should reconcile — not a functional bug since the backend/Stripe side correctly charges $49.
